UndefinedObject.summary: Variable not defined in current scope

hint.page: Bare variable names need the `context.` prefix: context.params, context.session, context.current_user, context.page, context.location, context.environment. If not a context property, check for a missing assign/graphql/function call.
hint.partial: Variables in partials/commands/queries must be passed explicitly. Add a {% doc %} block declaring the parameter: {% doc %} @param {object} var_name {% enddoc %}
hint.default: This variable is not defined in the current scope. Use `context.X` for built-in objects, or ensure it's assigned before use.

UndefinedObject.shopify_guidance: platformOS does not have Shopify theme objects. Use `{% graphql %}` to fetch data from your schema, access request data via `context.params`, and user data via `context.current_user`.

UnknownFilter.summary: Filter does not exist in platformOS Liquid

hint.default: This filter is not available in platformOS. Check for typos or Shopify-specific filters that don't exist here.

UnknownFilter.shopify_guidance: This is a Shopify-specific filter. platformOS uses different filters — check with `lookup (completions mode)` at the filter position to see available filters.

hint.default: Record fields: id, table, created_at, updated_at, user_id, external_id, properties_object, property(name: "x"). Custom properties: `property(name: "field")` NOT direct access. StringFilter: `{ value: "x" }` object syntax. PropertyFilter fields: value, contains, starts_with, ends_with, not_contains, not_starts_with, not_ends_with, value_in, not_value, not_value_in, range, case_sensitive (NO operator, NO like). Sort: `sort: [{ properties: [{ name: "field", order: DESC }] }]` — ASC/DESC unquoted. Pagination: `total_entries` NOT total_count. No relation traversal on records — use separate queries.

ConvertIncludeToRender.summary: {% include %} shares parent scope — prefer {% render %} for isolation

hint.default: Prefer `{% render %}` over `{% include %}`. Render has isolated scope — pass all needed variables explicitly: `{% render 'partial', var: value %}`. Exception: `{% include %}` is correct for module helpers that need scope sharing (e.g., `{% include 'modules/user/helpers/can_do_or_unauthorized' %}`).

This tag is deprecated. `hash_assign` → use `{% assign hash["key"] = "value" %}` or `{% assign hash.key = "value" %}`. `parse_json` → use `{% assign obj = { "key": "value" } %}` or `{% assign arr = [1, 2, 3] %}`. Check the error message for the specific replacement.

MissingPartial.summary: Referenced partial/command/query file does not exist

hint.default: Create the missing file. Partials: app/views/partials/ (referenced bare, e.g. 'cards/product'). Commands: app/lib/commands/ (referenced as 'commands/...'). Queries: app/lib/queries/ (referenced as 'queries/...'). Do NOT prepend 'lib/' to the call — 'function' paths resolve under app/lib/, so 'lib/commands/X' expands to 'app/lib/lib/commands/X' and never resolves. Do NOT remove the render/function tag to silence this error.

MissingRenderPartialArguments.summary: Required @param not passed at {% render %} call site

hint.default: Check the partial's {% doc %} block for @param declarations. Required: @param name {string}. Optional: @param [name] {string} (brackets = may be omitted). Each required parameter must be passed as a named argument: {% render 'partial', param_name: value %}.

NestedGraphQLQuery.summary: {% graphql %} inside a loop — N+1 query problem

hint.default: Move the {% graphql %} call BEFORE the loop. Use a batch/list query to fetch all data in one request instead of one query per iteration.

Fix the exact line reported. Common causes: missing `= 'path'` in graphql/function tags, unclosed HTML/Liquid blocks, mismatched quotes. Inside {% liquid %} blocks: each statement on its own line, no delimiters.

TranslationKeyExists.summary: Translation key not found in translation files

hint.default: Add the key to app/translations/en.yml. Use dot notation for scoping. If creating translation files as part of a multi-file plan, pass pending_translations from validate_intent to suppress this error for keys that will be written soon.

UnknownProperty.summary: Property cannot be verified on the given object

hint.page: Check the correct traversal path: graphql results use `result.records.results`, not `result.results`. Use lookup (hover mode) to verify available properties.
hint.partial: The linter can't track property types across file boundaries. Add a {% doc %} block: {% doc %} @param {object} var_name {% enddoc %} to declare the parameter type.
hint.default: The linter cannot verify this property exists. Check the object's traversal path, or add a {% doc %} @param declaration if in a partial/command.

HardcodedRoutes.summary: URL is hardcoded instead of using dynamic construction

hint.default: Avoid hardcoded URLs — they break when slugs change. Build URLs dynamically using page slugs and `context.params`: e.g., `{% assign url = '/products/' | append: product.id %}`.
Add `loading="lazy"` to tags for better performance.
Add `width` and `height` attributes to tags to prevent layout shift (CLS).

Initialize the variable first: `{% assign var = {} %}`, then set properties: `{% assign var["key"] = "value" %}`. Both hash_assign and parse_json are deprecated — use assign with hash literals and bracket/dot notation.
Create the missing asset in app/assets/. This check has a high false-positive rate for module assets.

MetadataParamsCheck.summary: Render call parameters don't match partial's declared metadata

hint.default: Read the target partial's metadata/doc block. Pass all required parameters with matching types. NEVER pass null for a typed parameter — use the matching empty value: '' for {string}, 0 for {number}, false for {boolean}, {} for {object}, [] for {array}. A parameter is required when declared without brackets: @param {string} name. Optional when wrapped: @param {string} [name].

ValidDocParamType.summary: Invalid type in @param declaration — types must be lowercase

hint.default: Doc param types are case-sensitive and must be lowercase: {string}, {number}, {boolean}, {object}, {array}, {untyped}. Common mistake: {String} or {Object} (capitalized) — use {string} or {object} instead.

try_catch.summary: Error handling with {% try %}...{% catch error %}...{% endtry %}
try_catch.notes: The `error` variable in {% catch error %} is automatically recognized by the linter — no {% doc %} declaration needed. Use for GraphQL calls, API calls, or any operation that might fail.

theme_render_rc.summary: Render a partial from theme search paths
theme_render_rc.notes: Like {% render %} but searches theme_search_paths for the partial. Used for theming/white-labeling. Accepts the same arguments as render.

liquid_doc.summary: Parameter documentation block for partials, commands, and queries

annotations.@param: {type} name - description. Types (MUST be lowercase): string, number, boolean, object, array, untyped. WRONG: {String}, {Object}. RIGHT: {string}, {object}. NEVER pass null for a typed parameter — use the matching empty value. Required: @param {string} name. Optional: @param {string} [name].
annotations.@prompt: Natural language description of the partial's purpose (used by AI agents for discovery).
annotations.@example: Usage example showing how to render/call this partial.
annotations.@description: Detailed description of behavior and side effects.

liquid_doc.notes: NEVER pass null for a typed parameter — use the matching empty value: '' for {string}, 0 for {number}, false for {boolean}, {} for {object}, [] for {array}. Required param: @param {string} name. Optional param: @param {string} [name].

graphql_field_completions.summary: GraphQL field completions and hover docs in .graphql files
graphql_field_completions.notes: The LSP provides field-level completions and hover documentation inside .graphql files. Use lookup (completions mode) and lookup (hover mode) in GraphQL files to discover available fields, types, and query structure.

hash_literals.summary: Hash (object) literals in {% assign %} — replaces {% parse_json %}
hash_literals.migration:
Old: {% parse_json my_hash %}{"name": "John"}{% endparse_json %}
New: {% assign my_hash = { "name": "John" } %}
hash_literals.notes: Supports nested structures with hashes and arrays. parse_json is deprecated — use assign with hash literals instead.

array_literals.summary: Array literals in {% assign %} — direct initialization
array_literals.replaces: {% assign arr = '' | split: ',' %} and {% parse_json arr %}[...]{% endparse_json %}
array_literals.migration:
Old: {% parse_json arr %}["apple", "banana"]{% endparse_json %}
New: {% assign arr = ["apple", "banana"] %}
array_literals.notes: Direct array initialization replaces filter-dependent approaches. Supports mixed types.

assign_bracket_dot.summary: Enhanced assign with bracket and dot notation — replaces {% hash_assign %}
assign_bracket_dot.migration:
Old: {% hash_assign my_hash["key"] = "value" %}
New: {% assign my_hash["key"] = "value" %}
assign_bracket_dot.notes: Bracket and dot notation work interchangeably. hash_assign is deprecated — use assign with bracket/dot notation instead.

array_append_operator.summary: Array append operator (<<) for adding elements to arrays
array_append_operator.replaces: Workarounds using concat filter or parse_json manipulation
array_append_operator.notes: Appends a single element to an existing array. The array must be initialized first.

string_interpolation.summary: String interpolation in double-quoted strings using {{ }} syntax
string_interpolation.syntax:
{% assign greeting = "Hello {{ context.current_user.name }}!" %}
{% assign url = "/api/{{ context.params.id }}/details" %}
string_interpolation.notes: Double-quoted strings support {{ }} interpolation. Single-quoted strings remain literal (no interpolation). Must enable string_interpolation: true in configuration.

parse_json_deprecation.summary: {% parse_json %} is deprecated — use {% assign %} with hash/array literals
parse_json_deprecation.notes: parse_json is replaced by assign with hash/array literals. The new syntax is cleaner, supports nesting, and has better type safety.

hash_assign_deprecation.summary: {% hash_assign %} is deprecated — use {% assign %} with bracket/dot notation
hash_assign_deprecation.notes: hash_assign is replaced by the enhanced assign tag. Both bracket notation and dot notation are supported.

filter_argument_rules.summary: Common filter argument patterns — the linter checks filter names but NOT argument syntax

rules.map: One positional arg (property name): {{ items | map: "price" }}. Does NOT accept named args.
rules.sort: One optional positional arg: {{ items | sort: "name" }}. Does NOT accept named args like order:.
rules.where: 1-2 positional args: {{ items | where: "status", "active" }}. Does NOT accept named args.
rules.default: 1 positional arg + optional allow_false: {{ var | default: "fallback", allow_false: true }}.
rules.t: Named args only: {{ "key" | t: name: "value" }}. Translation key goes before the pipe.

filter_argument_rules.notes: pos-cli check validates filter names but not argument patterns. pos-supervisor adds structural warnings for known misuse patterns (wrong number of args, named args where not supported).

pages.rule: Pages are controllers — logic only, no inline HTML. Use graphql/render/function/redirect_to tags.

gotchas.message: In pages, use `context.params`, `context.session`, `context.current_user`, `context.page` — bare variable names are not valid.
Move {% graphql %} calls outside loops. Fetch data once, then iterate over results.
Prefer {% render %} over {% include %}. Render has isolated scope. Exception: {% include %} is correct for module helpers that need scope sharing (e.g., authorization helpers).
Page title: use `metadata: { title: 'Static Title' }` in front matter for static titles, or `{% content_for 'title' %}Dynamic Title{% endcontent_for %}` for data-dependent titles.

Slugs use `:param` for dynamic segments (NOT `[param]`, `{param}`, or `<param>`). No leading `/`. For the home page (root `/`), omit the slug entirely — `app/views/pages/index.html.liquid` serves `/` by default. Examples: `products/:id`, `users/:user_id/orders/:id`, `search(/:query)`.
{% try %}...{% catch error %}...{% endtry %} — the `error` variable is auto-recognized, no declaration needed. Use for GraphQL/API calls that might fail.
{% parse_json %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign obj = { "key": "value" } %}` for hashes and `{% assign arr = [1, 2, 3] %}` for arrays.
{% hash_assign %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign hash["key"] = "value" %}` or `{% assign hash.key = "value" %}` instead.

partials.rule: Partials receive ONLY explicitly passed variables — no outer scope leaks. Do NOT run {% graphql %} in rendering partials.

gotchas.message: Declare all parameters with {% doc %} block: {% doc %} @param {type} name {% enddoc %}. The caller must pass them via {% render 'partial', name: value %}.

Do NOT run {% graphql %} in rendering partials (UI components). Fetch data in the page/command and pass results via render parameters. Note: query wrappers under app/lib/queries/ and commands under app/lib/commands/ CAN use {% graphql %} — invoke them via {% function r = 'queries/...' %} / {% function r = 'commands/...' %} (no `lib/` prefix in the call).
Required @param arguments must be passed at every {% render %} call site. Check the partial's {% doc %} block.
Partials have isolated scope. Every variable used must be passed explicitly or assigned locally.
NEVER pass null for a typed parameter — use the matching empty value: '' for {string}, 0 for {number}, false for {boolean}, {} for {object}, [] for {array}.
{% parse_json %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign obj = { "key": "value" } %}` for hashes and `{% assign arr = [1, 2, 3] %}` for arrays.
{% hash_assign %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign hash["key"] = "value" %}` or `{% assign hash.key = "value" %}` instead.

graphql.rule: Custom properties use property(name: "field") or properties_object, NOT direct field access. StringFilter requires object syntax.

gotchas.message: Record only has: id, table, created_at, updated_at, user_id, external_id, property(name: "x"), properties_object. Custom properties: `property(name: "field")` NOT direct access. No relation traversal — use separate queries.
StringFilter is an object: `table: { value: "name" }`, NOT a bare string.

PropertyFilter fields: `value`, `contains`, `starts_with`, `ends_with`, `not_contains`, `not_starts_with`, `not_ends_with`, `value_in`, `not_value`, `not_value_in`, `range`, `case_sensitive`. There is NO `operator`, NO `like` field.
Sort by custom properties: `sort: [{ properties: [{ name: "field", order: DESC }] }]`. Built-in: `sort: [{ created_at: { order: DESC } }]`. ASC/DESC are unquoted enum values, NOT strings.
Pagination fields: `total_entries` (NOT total_count), `results`, `has_next_page`, `has_previous_page`. Use `page` and `per_page` arguments.

commands.rule: Commands use build/check/execute pattern. Pages invoke commands via {% function %}, not {% graphql %} directly. Commands themselves may call {% graphql %} internally.

gotchas.message: Follow build → check → execute pattern: _build creates data, _check validates (MUST NOT execute if errors), _execute runs side effects.

Pages invoke commands via {% function result = 'commands/name/execute', arg: value %} (no `lib/` prefix — `function` paths resolve under app/lib/). Do not call {% graphql %} mutations directly from pages — use commands.
Command parameters must be declared with {% doc %} @param blocks. The caller passes them via {% function %}.
{% parse_json %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign obj = { "key": "value" } %}` for hashes and `{% assign arr = [1, 2, 3] %}` for arrays.
{% hash_assign %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign hash["key"] = "value" %}` or `{% assign hash.key = "value" %}` instead.

queries.rule: Queries live in app/lib/queries/. Pages invoke via {% function %}, not {% graphql %} directly. Query wrappers themselves call {% graphql %} internally.

gotchas.message: Pages invoke queries via {% function result = 'queries/name', arg: value %} (no `lib/` prefix — `function` paths resolve under app/lib/). Queries are read-only; all writes go through commands.
Query parameters must be declared with {% doc %} @param blocks.
{% parse_json %} is deprecated. Use `{% assign obj = { "key": "value" } %}` for hashes and `{% assign arr = [1, 2, 3] %}` for arrays.

translations.rule: Translation files are YAML in app/translations/. Access via {{ 'key' | t }} or {{ 'key' | t: name: value }}.

gotchas.message: Add missing translation key to app/translations/en.yml. Use dot notation for scoping. Pluralization: define one:/other: sub-keys.
Use t_escape filter for user-provided interpolation values: {{ 'key' | t_escape: name: user_input }}.

layouts.rule: Layouts render page body with {{ content_for_layout }} (required, exactly once). Named slots use {% yield 'name' %} (layout) paired with {% content_for 'name' %} (page).

gotchas.message: Access page metadata: {{ context.page.metadata.title }}. Set in page front matter under metadata: key.
Named layout slots: {% yield 'slot_name' %} (layout) / {% content_for 'slot_name' %}...{% endcontent_for %} (page). Page body renders via {{ content_for_layout }}.

schema.rule: Schema files define table structure — NOT GraphQL types. Access custom properties via properties_object.

gotchas.message: Schema uses `properties:` (not `fields:`). Property types: string, text, integer, float, boolean, datetime, date, array, upload.
Use CRUD generator for new resources: `pos-cli generate run modules/core/generators/crud resource_name prop:type --include-views`. Do NOT create CRUD files manually.

config.rule: app/config.yml controls application flags — changes require deploy.

gotchas.message: Use secure defaults: `escape_output_instead_of_sanitize: true`, `safe_translate: true`, `slug_exact_match: true`.
String interpolation ({{ }} in double-quoted strings) requires `string_interpolation: true` in app/config.yml. Single-quoted strings remain literal.

design-system.rule: Use only defined CSS classes and token variables. No inline styles. Load CSS via asset_url filter in layout. See domain_guide(domain: "design-system") for setup.

gotchas.message: Load design system CSS with asset_url filter: {{ "design-tokens.css" | asset_url }}. Never hardcode /assets/ paths — they break on deploy.

No inline style= attributes. Use design system classes (.card, .btn, .btn-primary, .container, .section, .layout-grid, .col-4/6/8/12, .feature-grid, .hero) or define overrides in {% content_for "head" %} using token variables.
.col-* classes must be inside a .layout-grid wrapper. Standalone .col-* has no effect.
var(--color-accent) is for interactive elements only (hover, focus, CTA buttons). Never use it for body text or static backgrounds.

content_triggers.message: Session data: `context.session` is set server-side via `{% session %}` tag but often derived from user input. NEVER trust session values for authorization — always re-validate. Use `context.current_user` for identity.
Forms: platformOS handles CSRF automatically for POST/PUT/DELETE pages. Ensure `method: post` is in front matter. For AJAX forms, include the CSRF token from `context.authenticity_token`.
XSS risk: `| raw` disables HTML escaping. Only use on trusted content (your own HTML, never user input). Prefer `| sanitize` for user content.

Cache: `{% cache key, expire: 3600 %}...{% endcache %}`. Key must be unique per content variant. Cache is per-instance, cleared on deploy. Never cache user-specific content without including user ID in the key.
Redirects: use `{% redirect_to '/path' %}` or `{% redirect_to '/path', status: 301 %}`. For static redirects, set `redirect_to` and `redirect_code` in front matter. Avoid redirect chains.
Assets: `| asset_url` resolves files from `app/assets/`. The URL is fingerprinted automatically on deploy for cache busting.

Background jobs: `{% background source_name: 'name' %}...{% endbackground %}`. Code inside runs asynchronously after the response. Useful for emails, API calls, heavy processing. Cannot return data to the page.

External API calls: `{% api_call 'config_name', url: url %}...{% endapi_call %}` or use the `api_call_send` GraphQL mutation. Wrap in `{% background %}` for non-blocking calls. Always handle errors with `{% try %}`.
Logging: `{% log message, type: 'info' %}`. Types: debug, info, warn, error. View logs with `pos-cli logs`. Use for debugging — remove excessive logging before deploy.

Layout slots: `{% content_for 'slot_name' %}...{% endcontent_for %}` in pages injects into `{% yield 'slot_name' %}` in the layout. The page body itself renders via `{{ content_for_layout }}` in the layout (not a named slot).
JSON responses: for API endpoints, use `.json.liquid` file extension and `format: json` in front matter. Then `{{ data | json }}` to serialize. Set `layout: ''` to disable HTML layout wrapping.
Shopify pattern: `shop.*` does not exist in platformOS. Use `context.constants.*` or custom schema properties.
Shopify pattern: `customer.*` does not exist in platformOS. Use `{% function current_user = 'modules/user/queries/user/current' %}` then `current_user.*`.
Shopify pattern: `cart.*` does not exist in platformOS. Cart requires a custom schema + GraphQL queries.
Shopify pattern: direct `product.*` access does not exist in platformOS. Use a custom schema + GraphQL query.
Shopify pattern: `{% form %}` tag is not valid in platformOS. Use HTML `<form>` with `authenticity_token` hidden field.
Shopify pattern: `{% section %}` tag is not valid in platformOS. Use `{% render 'path/to/partial' %}` instead.
Shopify pattern: `{% paginate %}` tag is not valid in platformOS. Use manual pagination with `context.params.page` and GraphQL.
Shopify pattern: `money` filter is not in platformOS. Format prices manually: `value | divided_by: 100.0 | round: 2`.
Shopify pattern: `img_url` filter is not in platformOS. Use `asset_url` for static assets.

modules_missing_docs.description: Module partials that use {% comment %} instead of {% doc %} for parameter docs, causing the LSP to infer required params from usage and emit false-positive MetadataParamsCheck on calling files. Diagnostic-pipeline suppresses those errors; this list marks which module paths are KNOWN offenders so we can distinguish them from new ones that still need upstream fixes.
